methods.name: add
methods.description: Adds an existing appearance to the Favorites list

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: item
methods.description: Returns the specified Appearance using an index into the collection.

methods.name: itemById
methods.description: Returns the Appearance by it's internal unique ID.

methods.name: itemByName
methods.description: Returns the specified appearance using the name as seen in the user interface. This often isn't a reliable way of accessing a specific appearance because appearances are not required to be unique.

properties.name: count
properties.description: The number of Appearances in the collection.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

add.title: FavoriteAppearances.add Method
add.description: Adds an existing appearance to the Favorites list
add.return_description: Returns the Appearance added to the favorites list or null if the operation failed.

parameters.name: appearance
parameters.description: The appearance to be added to the favorites list. This can come from a Library or from a Design.

classType.title: FavoriteAppearances.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

item.title: FavoriteAppearances.item Method
item.description: Returns the specified Appearance using an index into the collection.
item.return_description: Returns the specified appearance or null if an invalid index is specified.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the appearance to return where the first item in the collection is 0.

itemById.title: FavoriteAppearances.itemById Method
itemById.description: Returns the Appearance by it's internal unique ID.
itemById.return_description: Returns the specified appearance or null if there isn't a matching ID.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the appearance to return.

itemByName.title: FavoriteAppearances.itemByName Method
itemByName.description: Returns the specified appearance using the name as seen in the user interface. This often isn't a reliable way of accessing a specific appearance because appearances are not required to be unique.
itemByName.return_description: Returns the specified appearance or null if there isn't a matching name.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the appearance to return,.

count.title: FavoriteAppearances.count Property
count.description: The number of Appearances in the collection.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = favoriteAppearances_var.count

isValid.title: FavoriteAppearances.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = favoriteAppearances_var.isValid

objectType.title: FavoriteAppearances.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:

propertyValue = favoriteAppearances_var.objectType
